sprint: Fix real user-reported clone failure -- thumbnail signed URL never converted to a real data URI
task: A third real clone bug: 'duplicated but thumbnail did not resolve' -- the Welcome-screen card for a fresh clone showed a broken-image icon. Root cause: ThemeRepositoryClient.load()'s assets map holds Supabase Storage signed URLs (expiring after 1 hour by design), and the materializer stored those URL strings directly as an asset's value instead of converting them to a real data: URI the way every other Project asset in this codebase is stored. A more serious latent bug found in the same investigation: ProjectCompiler.js's _toBlob only converts a data:-prefixed string via fetch, so a future re-Build of an affected clone would have silently corrupted every signed-URL asset it carried (byte-encoding the URL text itself as if it were the image's own pixels). Fixed at the root: _materializeProjectFromPackage now returns a Promise, downloading and converting every signed-URL asset to a real data: URI before the Project is ever opened or saved; a conversion failure is disclosed, not swallowed, leaving that one asset as its original URL rather than corrupting the clone. Added onerror fallbacks on the Welcome-screen card thumbnails as defense in depth. Verified via a new test serving a real HTTP endpoint standing in for a signed URL, confirming the saved clone's thumbnail/preview are real data: URIs. Full regression across 15 scripts + a new test + both goldenBuild.js suites unchanged. See CLAUDE.md's matching entry for the full account.
